replication_architecture.description: Yithian Archive and Great Library replication strategies

master_slave.description: One primary handles writes, replicas handle reads

master.role: Primary write node for current timeline

master_slave.failover: Automatic voting among secondaries if master fails
master_slave.use_case: Temporal consciousness database (Great Race of Yith)

sharding.description: Horizontal partitioning of data across multiple servers
sharding.routing: Hash of temporal coordinates determines shard
sharding.use_case: Scaling consciousness storage beyond single node capacity

peer_to_peer.description: Every node contains full copy, all can read/write
peer_to_peer.reason: Yithian database requires strong consistency; P2P causes write conflicts

synchronous.description: Master waits for slave acknowledgment before commit
synchronous.latency: High (round-trip to Pluto: 4-6 hours at light speed)

asynchronous.description: Master commits immediately, slaves catch up

snapshot.frequency: Daily at midnight (when stars are quiet)

incremental.destination: Replicated to Yuggoth via Mi-Go courier
